gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the Databricks benchmark job ownership inversion and found one document-root export leak: document_kv_cache root symbols still resolved through the legacy root. The root export map now redirects Databricks job symbols to document_kv_cache.databricks_job, tests cover document-root imports/star imports and legacy private-hook direct construction, and re-review approved the branch with no remaining findings.

issues.resolution: Added document-root export routing for Databricks job symbols, added root export assertions, and covered direct legacy _cluster_config_from_benchmark_job monkeypatch behavior.
issues.summary: document_kv_cache root exports still returned legacy Databricks job classes and functions after the ownership inversion.

scope: document Databricks benchmark job implementation ownership
legacy Databricks benchmark job compatibility wrapper
legacy module execution and helper monkeypatch migration hooks
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_databricks_job.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
python -m py_compile src/document_kv_cache/__init__.py src/document_kv_cache/databricks_job.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/databricks_job.py tests/test_databricks_job.py tests/test_public_package.py
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
PYTHONPATH=src poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.pr_evidence --validate-directory pr-evidence
repository secret scan over README, package metadata, source, tests, Databricks helpers, PR evidence, and GitHub metadata
GPT-5.5 review and re-review after resolving the document-root export finding
what_changed: Moved the Databricks V1 benchmark job implementation into document_kv_cache.databricks_job.
Routed document_kv_cache root Databricks job symbols to the document-owned module.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.databricks_job into a compatibility wrapper over the document module.

Preserved legacy module execution, direct writer monkeypatch behavior, public and private helper monkeypatch behavior, pickle support, slotted config layout, and the previous legacy star-import surface.
Recorded document ownership in public package tests and the legacy package README.
why: The generalized package should own the reusable Databricks AWS g5 cluster helper and V1 benchmark job entry point. Keeping legacy compatibility makes this an incremental, behavior-preserving step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
